kubectl get secret -n mongodb app-user-password -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d && echo
kubectl get secret -n mongodb app-user-password-next -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d && echo
kubectl get secret -n mongodb reporting-user-password -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d && echo
input.description: Decode all relevant passwords from secrets

content.content:
admin123password
appuser123password
newAppUserPassword456
reporting123password

content.thinking:
Now I have all the passwords:
- admin password: `admin123password`
- app-user current password: `appuser123password`
- app-user next password: `newAppUserPassword456`
- reporting password: `reporting123password`

Now I need to:
1. Update the app-user's password in MongoDB to the new password from `app-user-password-next`
2. Update the `app-user-password` secret to match the new password
3. Verify that reporting-user and admin access still work
4. Verify that data in `appdb.testdata` remains accessible
I need to identify which pod is serving as the primary in the replica set before making any changes.

content.text: Now I'll find the primary member of the replica set.

input.description: Find the primary member of the replica set
